words.meaning: The amount of work a team completes per sprint, measured in story points
words.example: Our average velocity is 40 story points per sprint.

words.meaning: The process of reviewing and refining the product backlog
words.example: In Thursday's grooming session, we'll review the tickets for next sprint.

words.meaning: A research task to explore a problem and reduce uncertainty before committing
words.example: Let's create a spike to investigate the best approach for the new caching layer.

words.meaning: The agreed checklist that must be satisfied for a task to be considered complete
words.example: Our definition of done requires code review, testing, and documentation.

words.meaning: The person responsible for defining what the team should build and in what order
words.example: The product owner approved the new feature for this sprint.

speaking.title: 💬 Speaking — Sprint Planning
speaking.context: Sprint planning is starting. The PM presents the top 5 tickets for the next sprint totaling 35 story points. Your team's average velocity is 40 points. She asks: 'Does the team feel comfortable committing to all 5 tickets for this sprint?' One ticket has unclear requirements. Another ticket depends on a third-party API that is not yet available.
speaking.prompt: Respond as a developer in the sprint planning. Raise your concerns about the unclear ticket and the external dependency. Propose how to handle them.
speaking.sample_answer: Thanks for presenting the sprint. I'm mostly comfortable with the plan, but I want to raise two concerns before we commit. First, Ticket 3 — the analytics dashboard — has acceptance criteria that mention 'real-time data' but we haven't defined what 'real-time' means exactly. Does that mean sub-second updates, or is every 5 minutes acceptable? The implementation is very different. Could we do a quick clarification session with the product owner this week before we commit to that one? Second, Ticket 5 depends on the Stripe webhook API that isn't ready yet. If there's any delay on their end, we'd be blocked. I'd suggest we either add a buffer or convert it to a spike to prepare the implementation so we're ready the moment the API is available. With those two addressed, I think we can confidently commit to the rest of the sprint.

reading.title: 📖 Reading — Effective Sprint Planning
reading.passage:
Sprint planning sets the tone for the entire sprint. A good sprint planning session results in a clear, realistic commitment that the whole team believes in. A bad one results in an overcommitted team that ships half of what was planned.

Before committing to any ticket, every team member should feel confident they understand it. If a ticket is unclear, do not commit to it — either clarify it in the meeting or move it to a future sprint. 'We'll figure it out as we go' is a red flag.

External dependencies are the biggest risk in sprint planning. If your work depends on another team, a third-party API, or a design that is not yet complete, flag it explicitly. Build in time for delays.

Avoid the trap of optimistic planning. Teams consistently underestimate complexity and overestimate their velocity. A sprint where you complete 100% of committed work feels far better than a sprint where you complete 70% — even if the absolute output is the same.

End every sprint planning session with a clear goal: a one-sentence description of the sprint's primary objective. This sprint goal keeps the team focused when priorities change mid-sprint.
questions: What should you do if a ticket is unclear during sprint planning?
Why do teams consistently underestimate complexity?
answers: Either clarify it in the meeting or move it to a future sprint — do not commit to unclear work.
Because people naturally assume the best case and underestimate unknowns and dependencies.

key_phrases.phrase: I'd like to flag a concern before we commit to this
key_phrases.meaning: Saya ingin menyampaikan kekhawatiran sebelum kita berkomitmen pada ini

key_phrases.phrase: This ticket has an external dependency on...
key_phrases.meaning: Tiket ini memiliki ketergantungan eksternal pada...

key_phrases.phrase: Can we clarify the acceptance criteria before we estimate this?
key_phrases.meaning: Bisakah kita mengklarifikasi kriteria penerimaan sebelum kita estimasi ini?

items.phrase: Before we commit to this, I want to flag that the requirements for this ticket are unclear.
items.indonesian: Sebelum kita berkomitmen pada ini, saya ingin menyampaikan bahwa persyaratan untuk tiket ini tidak jelas.

items.phrase: This ticket depends on the external API being ready — should we add a contingency?
items.indonesian: Tiket ini bergantung pada API eksternal yang siap — haruskah kita menambahkan rencana cadangan?

items.phrase: With those concerns addressed, I'm confident we can commit to this sprint.
items.indonesian: Dengan kekhawatiran tersebut diatasi, saya yakin kita bisa berkomitmen pada sprint ini.
